Title: A Study on the Cell Layer Patterns of a Citrus Periclinal Chimera Reveals β-Cryptoxanthin Regulation in Citrus Fruits

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hongrou Huyou (OCC); Owari satsuma mandarin (OOO); Changshan Huyou (CCC); mandarin fruits; Marsh grapefruit callus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary provitamin A; improves nutritional and aesthetic value of citrus fruits
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene to form β-cryptoxanthin in the carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as direct determinant of β-cryptoxanthin accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR; PCR-based sequencing; MYB107 overexpression and RNAi followed by BCH expression and β-cryptoxanthin measurement; yeast one-hybrid; dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with β-cryptoxanthin accumulation; higher in OOO > OCC > CCC in pulp at 195 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MYB107-overexpressing citrus calli and fruits; downregulated in MYB107-RNAi fruits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage/ripening; transient overexpression or RNAi in fruits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BCH transcript abundance was strongly positively associated with β-cryptoxanthin accumulation in citrus tissues. 2. MYB107 directly bound and activated the BCH promoter. 3. BCH expression increased in MYB107 overexpression systems and decreased after MYB107 interference, paralleling β-cryptoxanthin changes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful direct target for engineering higher β-cryptoxanthin in citrus; can be combined with MYB107-based activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper indicates MYB107 may specifically regulate BCH among tested carotenogenic genes.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hongrou Huyou/Owari satsuma mandarin/Changshan Huyou context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hongrou Huyou (OCC); Owari satsuma mandarin (OOO); Changshan Huyou (CCC); mandarin fruits; Marsh grapefruit callus
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary provitamin A; contributes to fruit nutritional quality and coloration
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates BCH promoter; broadly upregulates multiple carotenogenic genes in overexpression contexts
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical positive regulator of β-cryptoxanthin accumulation identified in both pulp and flavedo coexpression analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances carotenoid biosynthesis, fruit yellow/orange coloration, and β-cryptoxanthin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; yeast transactivation assay; subcellular localization; citrus callus overexpression; transient fruit overexpression; transient RNAi interference; HPLC carotenoid profiling; Y1H; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with β-cryptoxanthin accumulation; temporal increase during fruit development; in pulp and flavedo OOO > OCC > CCC; higher in citrus species with high β-cryptoxanthin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In overexpression calli and fruits, elevated MYB107 increases carotenoids and carotenogenic gene expression; in RNAi fruits, reduced MYB107 lowers β-cryptoxanthin and target gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage/ripening; dark treatment after agroinjection in transient fruit assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for subcellular localization; pH7WG2D overexpression construct; pK7GWIWG2D RNAi construct
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May also affect fruit degreening/chlorophyll degradation or chloro-chromoplast transition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified twice by WGCNA in pulp and flavedo; shown to have transcriptional activation activity in yeast; authors report negative results for direct activation of other tested carotenogenic gene promoters, suggesting specificity toward BCH
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB107 expression is strongly positively associated with β-cryptoxanthin accumulation across citrus tissues and species. 2. MYB107 is a nucleus-localized transcriptional activator. 3. Overexpression in citrus calli and fruits increases carotenoid accumulation and upregulates carotenogenic genes, while interference has opposite effects. 4. MYB107 directly binds to and activates the BCH promoter, promoting β-cryptoxanthin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering citrus with improved provitamin A content and fruit color; can be used to enhance β-cryptoxanthin via BCH activation.
